author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
hari hari! āra kabe pālaṭibe daśā
e⋅daśā kariyā bāme, jā’ba vṛndāvana⋅dhāme,
ei mane kariyāchi āśā

dhana⋅jana putra⋅dāre, esaba kariyā dūre,
ekānta hoiyā kabe jāba
saba duḥkha parihari’, vṛndāvane vāsa kari’,
mādhukarī māgiyā khāiba

jamunāra jala jena, amṛta samāna hena,
kabe piba udara pūriyā
kabe rādhā⋅kuṇḍa⋅jale, snāna kari’ kutūhale,
śyāma-kuṇḍe rahiba paḍiyā

bhramiba dvādaśa vane, rasakeli je je sthāne,
premāveśe gaḍāgaḍi diyā
śudhāiba jane jane, vrajavāsigaṇa⋅sthāne,
nivediba caraṇa dhariyā

bhajanera sthāna kabe, nayana-gocara habe,
āra jata āche upavana
tā’ra madhye vṛndāvana, narottama dāsera mana,
āśā kare jugala⋅caraṇa
en_translation:
Hari Hari! When will my condition change? Having left this state behind, I will go to the abode of Vṛndāvana—this is the hope I have cherished in my mind.

Abandoning wealth, followers, children, and wife far away, when will I go, becoming single-minded? Giving up all distress, residing in Vṛndāvana, I will eat by begging alms.

When will I fill my belly drinking the water of the Yamunā, which is like nectar? When, bathing with curiosity in the waters of Rādhā-kuṇḍa, will I lie down at Śyāma-kuṇḍa?

I will wander in the twelve forests, in all the places of playful pastimes, rolling on the ground in the ecstasy of love. I will ask each and every person at the places of the residents of Vraja, and I will submit my petition holding their feet.

When will the place of worship become visible to my eyes, along with all the gardens that exist? Among them is Vṛndāvana; Narottama Dāsa's mind hopes for the lotus feet of the Divine Couple.
